macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Spike to $120 Triggers Global Market Selloff
items.summary: Oil prices surged to nearly $120 per barrel on March 9, 2026, causing widespread stock market declines. The Dow Jones fell 721 points, S&P 500 dropped 1.3%, and Asian markets experienced steeper losses with Japan's Nikkei 225 plunging 5.3% and South Korea's KOSPI crashing 6%.

items.title: Trump Signals Iran Conflict 'Very Complete,' Oil Tumbles
items.summary: President Trump indicated the Iran military operation is 'very complete' and 'very far' ahead of schedule, triggering a reversal in market sentiment. Oil prices dropped as much as 10%, equity futures climbed across Asia, and the S&P 500 rose 0.8% on Monday after intraday losses.

items.title: S&P 500 Breaks Below 6800 Support Level
items.summary: The S&P 500 has broken through the 6800 support level as oil volatility continues. Markets experienced a 20% oil spike over the weekend followed by a sharp reversal, with analysts noting the peak of an 18-year market cycle is approaching.

items.title: Market Dispersion Trade Unwinds Amid Volatility
items.summary: The dominant market theme of low correlation and high dispersion among individual stocks has unwound significantly over recent days. Options traders are managing short-term risk through new Monday and Wednesday expiration cycles as VIX volatility signals increase.

items.title: Utilities Emerge as Flight-to-Safety Sector
items.summary: As broad market indexes correct, utilities have significantly outperformed the S&P 500 and are continuing to rise. Investment strategists are raising cash and positioning defensively while hunting for bargains in oversold sectors like software.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $70K Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin slipped back below $70,000 following a brief push higher, amid volatile markets driven by war tensions, oil shocks, and weak jobs data. Recession fears persist with J.P. Morgan estimating a 40% probability of a U.S. and global downturn in 2026.

items.title: CLARITY Act Nears Final Countdown
items.summary: The CLARITY Act is in its final stages, amid ongoing disputes between banks and crypto firms over stablecoin yields. This regulatory development is drawing significant attention in the crypto space.

items.title: Vancouver Abandons Bitcoin-Friendly City Proposal
items.summary: Vancouver city council is set to quietly drop Mayor Ken Sim's proposal to make the city bitcoin-friendly, marking a retreat from earlier ambitions.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Strong Despite War Fears and Oil Surge
items.summary: Bitcoin remains resilient above key support levels despite rising war risks with Iran, surging oil prices, and inflation concerns, with analysts noting unusual relative strength and potential relief rally if tensions ease.

items.title: Ethereum Staking Demand Surges as Validators Queue Grows
items.summary: Ethereum validator queue is growing vertically with increased staking demand, alongside network effects and credible neutrality attracting Wall Street interest.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: USC Researchers Develop AI That Self-Corrects Knowledge Gaps in Real Time
items.summary: USC Viterbi researchers created a method enabling AI models to dramatically improve performance on untrained data by iteratively recompiling and using objective feedback, achieving up to 96% accuracy. The approach has potential applications in mathematical reasoning and legal logic.[1]

items.title: NVIDIA's 2026 State of AI Report: Enterprises Shift to ROI-Focused AI Deployments
items.summary: NVIDIA's latest report reveals companies treating AI infrastructure as essential business technology, prioritizing measurable returns amid record-high spending and economic pressures to justify investments.[2]

items.title: LabVantage Launches CORTEX: AI-Powered Platform for Autonomous Lab Operations
items.summary: LabVantage Solutions introduced LabVantage CORTEX, integrating AI, analytics, and automation into its LIMS platform to enable worksheet assistance, sample management, and predictive quality control in labs.[3]

items.title: UCLA Hosts Researchers on AI's Emerging Role in Math and Physics Discovery
items.summary: Mathematicians, physicists, and AI experts gathered at UCLA, supported by OpenAI, to discuss how AI tools aid in exploring conjectures, verifying results, and accelerating research in complex mathematical and theoretical physics domains.[4]

items.title: ADLINK Partners with Noble Machines for Edge AI in Heavy-Duty Robotics
items.summary: ADLINK announced a strategic alliance with Noble Machines to develop high-performance edge AI platforms using NVIDIA Jetson Thor for general-purpose robots in harsh environments like mining and construction.[5]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Samsung Expands Glasses-Free 3D Gaming Library to 120+ Titles by End of 2026
items.summary: Samsung announced at GDC 2026 that its Odyssey 3D gaming ecosystem will expand to over 120 titles by year-end, with critically acclaimed games Hell Is Us and Cronos: The New Dawn arriving in March as part of the first wave of new 3D-enabled titles.

items.title: Crimson Desert Set for March Launch with HDR10+ GAMING Support
items.summary: Pearl Abyss's open-world action-adventure game Crimson Desert will launch in March featuring Samsung's HDR10+ GAMING technology for a premium visual experience.

items.title: IGN Entertainment Named Exclusive Media Sponsor for Nite to Unite 2026
items.summary: IGN Entertainment is serving as the exclusive media partner for the Nite to Unite 2026 fundraiser, which supports the ESA Foundation's initiatives to promote diversity in game development through scholarships and industry networking.

items.title: Major Mobile Game Releases This Week Include Subnautica: Below Zero and Nova Drift
items.summary: Multiple mobile games are launching in March 2026, with Subnautica: Below Zero and Nova Drift arriving on March 10, while Tom Clancy's The Division Resurgence is scheduled for March 31 across iOS and Android platforms.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tesla Director Thomas Dmytryk Departs After 11 Years
items.summary: Thomas Dmytryk, key architect of Tesla's over-the-air update system and Robotaxi software infrastructure, announced his departure after over a decade, citing family priorities in a LinkedIn post titled 'Why Now'. His work supported Tesla's ride-hailing service, charging network, battery business, and global operations.

items.title: Asian Computer Industry Online Exhibition 2026 Launches Today
items.summary: The ACI 2026 opens on March 10, 2026, running through June 30 as a four-month trade event connecting Asian suppliers, importers, and exporters with products like computer components, networking gear, monitors, and peripherals from Taiwanese manufacturers.
